ai_summary.summary: The Pennsylvania Department of Revenue released a collection of press releases and announcements covering tax collection reports, the Property Tax/Rent Rebate Program, and new tax credits including the Working Pennsylvanians Tax Credit. The department distributed rebates to seniors and people with disabilities, expanded program eligibility and income limits, extended filing deadlines, and launched outreach efforts in multiple languages. The administration also introduced new online tools, a 'Fast File' initiative, and reported that state revenue collections exceeded original estimates by $1.1 billion for fiscal year 2025-26.

Department of Transportation
Pennsylvania Department of Transportation
PennDOT supports and manages the Commonwealth's vast transportation network, helping to enhance the quality of life in our communities and keep PA's economy moving.
Report a State Road Concern
Find Driver & Vehicle Services
PennDOT is Working for You
At PennDOT, we oversee programs and policies affecting highways, urban and rural public transportation, airports, railroads, ports, and waterways. More than three-quarters of PennDOT's annual budget is invested in Pennsylvania's approximately 122,000 miles of state and local highways and 32,000 state and local bridges. We are directly responsible for 40,000 miles of highway and over 25,400 bridges, a system first established in 1911. We also administer the state's 11.8 million vehicle registrations and 10.2 million driver's licenses and IDs, and oversee safety and emission inspection programs.
Whether it's at a maintenance garage or a driver's license center, PennDOT's nearly 12,000 employees are focused on making improvements, enhancing equity in our transportation systems for all people, and striving for a safer, more connected Pennsylvania.

It lists the agency's organizational structure, programs, resources, and ongoing transportation projects across Pennsylvania. No specific legislative action, statement, or event is described.

title: Bonner Announces More than $3.5 Million Coming to the 17th District from Marcellus Shale Impact Fees
raw_text:
Bonner Announces More than $3.5 Million Coming to the 17th District from Marcellus Shale Impact Fees
June 16, 2026
HARRISBURG – Rep. Tim Bonner (R-Mercer/Butler) today announced municipalities in the 17th Legislative District will receive more than $656,698 in Marcellus Shale Impact Fees. In addition to the municipal funding, Mercer County will receive more than $313,261 and Butler County will receive more than $2.55 million from the impact fees.
“Pennsylvania is a leading producer of natural gas,” Bonner said. “This part of the economy cannot be ignored. We are fortunate to have abundant clean energy in our Commonwealth that supports infrastructure, public safety, national defense and other community priorities.”
The following impact fee disbursements in the 17th District were announced:
Mercer County:
•	Coolspring Township - $3,696.
•	Deer Creek Township - $749.
•	Delaware Township - $4,943.
•	East Lackawannock Township - $10,969.
•	Fairview Township - $6,030.
•	Findley Township - $4,858.
•	Fredonia Borough - $604.
•	French Creek Township - $2,993.
•	Grove City Borough - $3,608.

ai_summary.summary: Rep. Tim Bonner announced that municipalities in Pennsylvania's 17th Legislative District will receive over $3.5 million in Marcellus Shale Impact Fees, which are fees collected from natural gas drilling companies and distributed to local governments under a 2012 state law. The funds are allocated to Mercer County, Butler County, and individual townships and boroughs within the district. Bonner did not author the legislation or secure the funding — the disbursements are automatic under the existing Act 13 of 2012 formula.

title: Continue Reading
raw_text:
Bonner Announces More than $3.5 Million Coming to the 17th District from Marcellus Shale Impact Fees
June 16, 2026
HARRISBURG – Rep. Tim Bonner (R-Mercer/Butler) today announced municipalities in the 17th Legislative District will receive more than $656,698 in Marcellus Shale Impact Fees. In addition to the municipal funding, Mercer County will receive more than $313,261 and Butler County will receive more than $2.55 million from the impact fees.
“Pennsylvania is a leading producer of natural gas,” Bonner said. “This part of the economy cannot be ignored. We are fortunate to have abundant clean energy in our Commonwealth that supports infrastructure, public safety, national defense and other community priorities.”
The following impact fee disbursements in the 17th District were announced:
Mercer County:
•	Coolspring Township - $3,696.

title: Bonner Announces Safety Grants for Two Mercer County Schools
raw_text:
Bonner Announces Safety Grants for Two Mercer County Schools
June 16, 2026
HARRISBURG – Rep. Tim Bonner (R-Mercer/Butler) today announced that two nonpublic schools in the 17th Legislative District will receive state grants to enhance school safety, security and student well-being.
The schools receiving funding are:
•	Grove City Christian Academy – $21,000.
•	Stoneboro Wesleyan Methodist School – $75,000.
“Our students deserve safety and security when they walk into school,” Bonner said. “These grants will help them learn in an environment where they feel protected and supported. I am glad to see this funding go toward strengthening security measures and expanding resources that promote student well-being and peace of mind for families.”
The awards were approved by the Pennsylvania Commission on Crime and Delinquency (PCCD) School Safety and Security Committee as part of a statewide distribution of 353 grants totaling nearly $20 million.
Funding may be used for a range of school safety improvements, including security enhancements, emergency preparedness planning, training and personnel support, and programs that address student mental and behavioral health needs.
The School Safety and Security Grant Program is administered by PCCD and supports schools and other eligible entities across Pennsylvania in strengthening security infrastructure and expanding access to resources that promote safe learning environments.
Representative Tim Bonner
17th Legislative District
Pennsylvania House of Representatives
Media Contact: Andrew Forgotch
717.772.9905
aforgotch@pahousegop.com
www.repbonner.com / Facebook.com/RepBonner

Bonner announced the grants but did not administer or vote on them; the awards were approved by a state commission.
